Container.Name: nudgarr
Container.Overview: Nudgarr keeps your Radarr and Sonarr libraries improving automatically running scheduled or on-demand sweeps across three pipelines: Cutoff Unmet quality upgrades, Backlog nudges for missing content, and CF Score searches for files below your custom format cutoff. Supports multiple Radarr and Sonarr instances independently, with per-instance enable/disable, overrides, tag and quality profile filters, and indexer-friendly rate limiting via cooldowns, batch size, sleep, and jitter. Features include skip-queued items, auto-exclusion of titles that never download (with auto-unexclude), confirmed import tracking with turnaround times, queue-depth skip, maintenance windows, backlog grace period, Apprise push notifications, and an Intel dashboard for lifetime stats. Full web UI with sidebar navigation — Sweep, Library (History, Imports, CF Score, Exclusions), and configuration — responsive on desktop and mobile in any browser.

Config.Name: Port
Config.Description: Change if 8085 is already in use.

Config.Name: Config Path
Config.Description: Path to your Nudgarr config and data files.

Config.Name: TZ
Config.Description: Timezone for cron schedule. e.g. America/New_York, Europe/London. If not set, defaults to UTC. See https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/List_of_tz_database_time_zones

Config.Name: PUID
Config.Description: User ID to run Nudgarr as. Match your Unraid user.

Config.Name: PGID
Config.Description: Group ID to run Nudgarr as. Match your Unraid group.

Config.Name: SECRET_KEY
Config.Description: Optional. A persistent key is automatically created and saved to /config/nudgarr-secret.key on first start. Only set this if you want to manage the key yourself.
